events.desc: First known suspected case — a health worker — reports onset of symptoms including fever, hemorrhaging, vomiting and intense malaise. The case died at a medical centre in Bunia.
WHO alerted of a high-mortality outbreak of unknown illness in Mongbwalu Health Zone, Ituri Province, DRC. Four health workers died within 4 days.
Rapid response team investigation in Mongbwalu and Rwampara health zones confirms Bundibugyo virus disease (BVD) caused by Bundibugyo virus (BDBV).
INRB Kinshasa analyzed 13 blood samples from Rwampara Health Zone, Ituri Province.

Laboratory analysis confirmed BVD in 8 of 13 samples. DRC Ministry of Health officially declares the 17th Ebola outbreak. 246 suspected cases and 80 deaths across Rwampara, Mongbwalu, and Bunia. Uganda confirms first imported case from DRC — an elderly man admitted May 11, died May 14.

WHO Director-General declares a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) under IHR. Uganda confirms 2nd imported case from DRC. No licensed vaccine or therapeutics exist for Bundibugyo virus.
1 American healthcare worker exposed in DRC tests positive and is medically evacuated to Germany for treatment. 6 high-risk contacts also transferred to Germany for monitoring.
US CDC and DHS implement enhanced travel screening and entry restrictions. Africa CDC declares Public Health Emergency of Continental Security. Risk to US public remains low.
575 suspected cases and 148 deaths. 51 confirmed cases. Outbreak spreads to Nord-Kivu Province — cases reported in Goma. Over 1,000 contacts under follow-up in Ituri.

Outbreak expands to 3rd province — Sud-Kivu newly confirmed in a person who travelled from Tsopo Province. CDC reports 827 total cases (83 confirmed) and 176 suspected deaths. Uganda: 5 confirmed cases, 1 death.

ECDC reports over 650 suspected cases and 160 deaths, 64 confirmed cases across Ituri (60 confirmed) and Nord-Kivu (4 confirmed). Outbreak confirmed in Ituri, Nord-Kivu, and Sud-Kivu provinces. Uganda: 5 cases, 1 death — no local spread reported.

nodes.label: Rwampara HZ
nodes.detail: 6 health areas affected · confirmed outbreak origin

nodes.label: Mongbwalu HZ
nodes.detail: 3 health areas affected · 4 HCWs dead by May 9

nodes.label: Bunia HZ
nodes.detail: Epicentre hub · 650+ suspected cases · 160 deaths · 3 provinces confirmed

nodes.label: Kampala, Uganda
nodes.detail: 5 confirmed cases · 1 death · all traced to DRC travellers · no local spread

nodes.label: Germany
nodes.detail: 1 American HCW (confirmed case) + 6 high-risk contacts evacuated · May 17

nodes.label: United States
nodes.detail: Enhanced screening at entry points · May 18 · no confirmed cases · risk assessed as low
